The Division Universe / Evidence-led archive

The Division Lore Archive

A source-led archive of The Division lore, characters, events, collectibles and documented real-world influences.

No source, no claimCanon and theory stay separateEvery page is versioned
22structured records22source reviewed
CoverageVerified counts and acknowledged gaps TimelineSourced sequence without invented dates MethodologyEvidence, canon and review rules Review queueRecords awaiting human approval CopyrightTranscript and quotation boundaries CorrectionsReport an error with evidence SourcesShared provenance registry

1 record

Diseases

Structured, source-linked records in this category.

disease

Green Poison

The engineered smallpox outbreak also called the Dollar Flu, whose Black Friday spread drives the collapse depicted across The Division franchise.

not applicablesource reviewed

5 records

Events

Structured, source-linked records in this category.

event

Black Tusk Siege of Washington

The endgame invasion in which Black Tusk enters Washington, places the city under siege and reveals its campaign against SHD.

not applicablesource reviewed

event

Division Network Crisis

The nationwide SHD Network failure and Washington distress call that draw Division Agents to the capital at the beginning of The Division 2.

not applicablesource reviewed

event

Green Poison Outbreak

The Black Friday biological outbreak that breaks public order, overwhelms response systems and begins the central crisis of The Division universe.

not applicablesource reviewed

event

Lower Manhattan Biological Attack

The attack that kills civilians and SHD Agents in Lower Manhattan and triggers the Warlords of New York hunt for Aaron Keener.

not applicablesource reviewed

event

SHD Activation

The emergency activation of secretly trained Division Agents after the Green Poison outbreak overwhelms ordinary response systems.

not applicablesource reviewed

7 records

Factions

Structured, source-linked records in this category.

faction

Black Tusk

An advanced private military faction that sabotages the Division Network, invades Washington and pursues national control through high-end weapons and robotics.

not applicablesource reviewed

faction

Cleaners

A faction formed from sanitation workers and first responders who attempt to eradicate infection through fire.

not applicablesource reviewed

faction

Hyenas

A hostile Washington faction recognizable by gas masks and, in later seasonal material, an expanding Spice operation tied to the Dark Zones.

not applicablesource reviewed

faction

Outcasts

A hostile Division 2 faction whose continued activity and developing alliance with the True Sons and Hyenas are confirmed in official seasonal material.

not applicablesource reviewed

faction

Peacekeepers

A friendly Lower Manhattan survivor group formed by civilians and former Joint Task Force members.

not applicablesource reviewed

faction

Rikers

A faction originating with escaped Rikers Island prisoners and later recruits, reorganized into splinter groups after the first game.

not applicablesource reviewed

faction

True Sons

An organized paramilitary faction using former military and JTF expertise, heavy firepower and territorial control in Washington.

not applicablesource reviewed

2 records

Locations

Structured, source-linked records in this category.

location

Lower Manhattan

The New York setting of Warlords of New York, divided among returning factions and rogue Agents during the hunt for Aaron Keener.

not applicablesource reviewed

location

Washington, D.C.

The primary setting of The Division 2, where institutional collapse, civilian reconstruction and competing factions converge around the U.S. capital.

not applicablesource reviewed

2 records

Organizations

Structured, source-linked records in this category.

organization

Joint Task Force

The first-responder organization commonly called the JTF, which attempts to contain the outbreak before SHD activation and later contributes veterans to survivor groups.

not applicablesource reviewed

organization

Strategic Homeland Division

The secret emergency organization commonly called the Division or SHD, whose trained civilian Agents are activated when national systems fail.

not applicablesource reviewed

3 records

People

Structured, source-linked records in this category.

person

Aaron Keener

A first-wave Division Agent who went rogue and became the central target of the Warlords of New York manhunt.

not applicablesource reviewed

person

Gordon Amherst

The virologist identified by the Division as the creator of Green Poison.

not applicablesource reviewed

person

Vitaly Tchernenko

A Russian virologist pursued by multiple factions because his expertise could support mass production of a broad-spectrum antiviral.

not applicablesource reviewed

2 records

Real-world influences

Structured, source-linked records in this category.

government directive

Directive 51 (NSPD-51 / HSPD-20)

A real 2007 United States continuity directive that The Division uses as the fictional legal foundation for the Strategic Homeland Division.

confirmed influencesource reviewed

real world event

Operation Dark Winter

A 2001 senior-level tabletop exercise built around a fictional smallpox attack, and a documented real-world influence on the premise of The Division.

confirmed influencesource reviewed

Unofficial, source-led research into The Division universe and its real-world influences.
